Patch Set 2:

I attempted to fix the build errors present in this patch and succeeded. Yay! Now, keep in mind I barely have any idea what I am doing, so please doublecheck the changes I made to make sure they make sense.

This service is only provided in hopes to be useful and reduce latency from push to getting actual results in the face of trivial code errors

Here's summary of my changes:
The change adds a missing `struct dentry *parent;` variable declaration in the `obd_pool_init()` function. This was necessary to resolve a compile error that occurred due to the use of the `parent` variable (likely in subsequent code not shown in the diff) without prior declaration. The addition ensures proper compilation by declaring the variable before it is used, fixing a compilation issue introduced in a previous patch.

This service is only provided in hopes to be useful and reduce latency from push to getting actual results in the face of trivial code errors

Here's summary of my changes:
The change adds a missing `struct dentry *parent;` variable declaration in the `obd_pool_init()` function in `lustre/obdclass/page_pools.c`. This declaration was necessary to resolve compile errors that arose in a previous patch, likely due to the use of the `parent` variable (e.g., for creating or managing debugfs entries) without it being declared. The addition ensures proper compilation by declaring the variable before it is used.

commit.subject: LU-18687 libcfs: move include to top-level
commit.message:
LU-18687 libcfs: move include to top-level

Move all headers to the new top-level include/linux/ directory.
This aligns the libcfs/, lnet/, and lustre/ directory structure
with the upstream kernel.

Test-Parameters: trivial
